data.title: Longest Continuous Increasing String
data.statement:
The standard English keyboard layout consists of 26 keys (one for each character of the English alphabet) arranged in the following manner:
```+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
|       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
|   Q   |   W   |   E   |   R   |   T   |   Y   |   U   |   I   |   O   |   P   |
|       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+
  |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
  |   A   |   S   |   D   |   F   |   G   |   H   |   J   |   K   |   L   |
  |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
      |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
      |   Z   |   X   |   C   |   V   |   B   |   N   |   M   |
      |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
      +-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+```A string is said to be <<continuous>> if every 2-character substring in the string is formed by <<physically adjacent keys>> on the keyboard. Two keys are <<physically adjacent>> if their sides intersect (refer to the diagram above). For example, the string "HYTGVFR" is continuous because each pair of keys "HY", "YT", "TG", "GV", "VF", and "FR" are physically adjacent.

A string is said to be <<increasing>> if the ASCII values of its characters are in <<increasing order>> from left to right. For example, the string "BFHRTZ" is increasing, while the strings "NPQXDCT" and "GHJNNPX" are not.

What is the <<longest continuous increasing string>> that <<begins with a given letter>> [[L]]?
data.inputDescription: <<Line 1>>: [[L]]; a single uppercase English alphabetical letter ({{A}}-{{Z}}).
data.outputDescription: <<Line 1>>: The longest continuous increasing string beginning with the letter [[L]].

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

The standard English keyboard layout consists of 26 keys (one for each character of the English alphabet) arranged in the following manner:
+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
|       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
|   Q   |   W   |   E   |   R   |   T   |   Y   |   U   |   I   |   O   |   P   |
|       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+-+-----+
  |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
  |   A   |   S   |   D   |   F   |   G   |   H   |   J   |   K   |   L   |
  |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+
      |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
      |   Z   |   X   |   C   |   V   |   B   |   N   |   M   |
      |       |       |       |       |       |       |       |
      +-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+-------+
A string is said to be continuous if every 2-character substring in the string is formed by physically adjacent keys on the keyboard. Two keys are physically adjacent if their sides intersect (refer to the diagram above). For example, the string "HYTGVFR" is continuous because each pair of keys "HY", "YT", "TG", "GV", "VF", and "FR" are physically adjacent.

A string is said to be increasing if the ASCII values of its characters are in increasing order from left to right. For example, the string "BFHRTZ" is increasing, while the strings "NPQXDCT" and "GHJNNPX" are not.

What is the longest continuous increasing string that begins with a given letter L?




   

      
Input

      
Line 1: L; a single uppercase English alphabetical letter (A-Z).

   

   

      
Output

      
Line 1: The longest continuous increasing string beginning with the letter L.

   

   

      
Constraints

      
None.

   

   

      
Example

      

         

            
Input

            
A

         

         

            
Output

            
ASXZ
